commented_code:
-# Pub/Sub
\ No newline at end of file
+# Pub/Sub
+
+Pub/Sub in Redis is a powerful messaging paradigm that allows for real-time communication between clients through a publish/subscribe model. In this system, publishers send messages to specific channels without knowing who, if anyone, will receive them. Subscribers, on the other hand, express interest in particular channels and receive messages published to those channels instantly. This decouples the message producers from the consumers, facilitating flexible and scalable communication. Key commands in this model include `PUBLISH` for sending messages, `SUBSCRIBE` for listening to channels, and `UNSUBSCRIBE` for stopping the reception of messages.

discussion_comments.comment_body:
```suggestion
# Pub/Sub

Pub/Sub in Redis is a powerful messaging paradigm that allows for real-time communication between clients through a publish/subscribe model. In this system, publishers send messages to specific channels without knowing who, if anyone, will receive them. Subscribers, on the other hand, express interest in particular channels and receive messages published to those channels instantly. This decouples the message producers from the consumers, facilitating flexible and scalable communication. Key commands in this model include `PUBLISH` for sending messages, `SUBSCRIBE` for listening to channels, and `UNSUBSCRIBE` for stopping the reception of messages.

Visit the following resources to learn more:

- [@official@Pub/Sub in Redis](https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/interact/pubsub/)
```

commented_code:
# Go realtime communication
+
+## What is realtime communication?
+Just as it says in the name, realtime communication is the handling of requests concurrently and efficiently. Whether it is a chat/messaging app, an email service, a game server or any collaborative online project (for example, Excalidraw).
+
+## How is realtime communication achieved?
+There are a few different ways of handling realtime communication, but the most common is through the use of websockets. Other options for handling realtime communications include MQTT protocol and server-sent events, among others.
+
+[Golang websocket](https://youtu.be/G8SKhZMqvsE)

discussion_comments.comment_body:
```suggestion
## What is real-time communication?
Just as it says in the name, real-time communication is the handling of requests concurrently and efficiently. Whether it is a chat/messaging app, an email service, a game server or any collaborative online project (for example, Excalidraw), there are a few different ways of handling real-time communication, but the most common is through the use of WebSockets. Other options for handling real-time communications include MQTT protocol and server-sent events, among others.

Learn more from the following resources:

- [@video@Golang websocket](https://youtu.be/G8SKhZMqvsE)
```
